title: trema lamarckianum
intro: Trema lamarckianum, also known as White Teak or White Ironbark, is a deciduous tree native to tropical regions of Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimalarial properties. The health benefits of Trema lamarckianum include its ability to reduce fever, treat skin infections, and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for medicinal use, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential interactions.
